ceb.BEF 1193–BET 1222 AND 1235: sa wala pa ang 1193–gikan sa 1222 hangtod sa 1235

zh.lifeSummary: Heinrich IV van Kessel graf於1193年之前出生。他於大約1205年與Udelhild Van Hengebach結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於從1222年到1235年。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Heinrich IV van Kessel graf于1193年之前出生。他于大约1205年与Udelhild Van Hengebach结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于从1222年到1235年。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Kessel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cornish: variant of   Kessell  .

German: from Middle High German keʒʒel ‘kettle, cauldron’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of copper cooking vessels, or alternatively a topographic and habitational name, from the same word in the sense ‘(ring-shaped) hollow’.
Dutch (mainly Van Kessel): habitational name for someone from any of the places so named in the Dutch provinces of North Brabant and Limburg or in the Belgian provinces of Antwerp and Limburg.

variants.name: Kettel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ewald, Kurt.  Scandinavian Morten.
North German: variant of   Kessel  .
English (Surrey and Kent): variant of   Kettle  .

variants.name: Kettle
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Ketel, Kitel (Old Norse Ketill, meaning ‘kettle, cauldron’, perhaps also ‘helmet’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Kessel  .
Irish: in Ireland, this generally is the English name in 1 above, but in some cases it may also be an Anglicized form of Mac Thorcaill (see McCorkell ).

variants.name: Kesselman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avraham, Dov, Meyer, Moshe, Shmuel.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a coppersmith or maker of copper cooking vessels, from German Kessel ‘kettle, cauldron’, with the addition of Mann (Yiddish man) ‘man’.

Americanized form of German Kesselmann: habitational name, with the addition of Middle High German man ‘man’, from any of several places called Kessel in Bavaria, Rhineland, Pomerania, or Silesia (see Kessel ).

variants.name: Kessell
meanings: Cornish (Cornwall and Devon): habitational name from one or more of several places in Cornwall whose names derive from Middle Cornish castell, kestell ‘castle, village, tor’, such as Kestle in Saint Columb Minor, Kestle in Egloshayle, and Kestal in Saint Hilary.
Altered form of German or Dutch   Kessel  .

nameMeanings.name: Heinrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from heim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power’. See Henry .

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Vivian   and   Vivien .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: Heinrich IV van Kessel graf was born before 1193. He married Udelhild Van Hengebach about 1205. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died from 1222 to 1235.

fr.lifeSummary: Heinrich IV van Kessel graf est né avant 1193. Il a épousé Udelhild Van Hengebach environ 1205. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé du 1222 au 1235.

de.lifeSummary: Heinrich IV van Kessel graf wurde vor 1193 geboren. Er hat um 1205 Udelhild Van Hengebach geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb von 1222 bis 1235.

it.lifeSummary: Heinrich IV van Kessel graf è nato il prima del 1193. Lui sposato Udelhild Van Hengebach circa 1205. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì da 1222 a 1235.

ja.lifeSummary: Heinrich IV van Kessel grafは1193年以前に生まれた。約1205年にUdelhild Van Hengebachと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼はから1222年まで1235年に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Heinrich IV van Kessel graf(은)는 1193년 이전 태어났다. 그는 대략 1205년 Udelhild Van Hengebach(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1222년 부터 1235년 까지 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Heinrich IV van Kessel graf nasceu antes 1193. Ele casou-se com Udelhild Van Hengebach aproximadamente 1205. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu de 1222 a 1235.

ru.lifeSummary: Heinrich IV van Kessel graf родился перед 1193 г. Он женился на женщине по имени Udelhild Van Hengebach около 1205 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер с 1222 по 1235.

sm.BEF 1193–BET 1222 AND 1235: a'e le'I oo le 1193–mai le 1222 e o'o I le 1235
sm.BET 1222 AND 1235: mai le 1222 e o'o I le 1235

es.lifeSummary: Heinrich IV van Kessel graf nació antes de 1193. Se casó con Udelhild Van Hengebach aproximadamente 1205. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió desde 1222 hasta 1235.
